Bluetooth reconnection control functions for pausing, resuming, and resetting intervals.

# Purpose
This C source code file defines a set of functions related to Bluetooth driver reconnection management. The functions [`bt_driver_reconnect_pause`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_pause>) and [`bt_driver_reconnect_resume`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_resume>) are likely intended to control the pause and resume states of the Bluetooth reconnection process. The function [`bt_driver_reconnect_try_now`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_try_now>) takes a boolean parameter `ignore_paused`, which suggests it attempts an immediate reconnection, potentially bypassing any paused state. The function [`bt_driver_reconnect_reset_interval`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_reset_interval>) is likely used to reset the reconnection interval to its initial state. Lastly, [`bt_driver_reconnect_notify_platform_bitfield`](<#bt_driver_reconnect_notify_platform_bitfield>) accepts a `uint32_t` parameter `platform_bitfield`, but its implementation is currently a placeholder, indicating no operation is performed with the input.
